title: betula papyrifera subsp. kenaica
intro: Betula papyrifera subsp. kenaica, also known as paper birch, is a subspecies of the paper birch tree (Betula papyrifera) that is native to North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin issues. The health benefits of Betula papyrifera subsp. kenaica include reducing fever, alleviating sore throats, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and poultices that can be made from the bark, leaves, or resin, although more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these preparations.
